top_signals.title: Warsh May Tackle Inflation in New Way: Fmr. Fed Governor
top_signals.summary: Former Federal Reserve Governor and former Wells Fargo chair Betsy Duke analyzed the recent inflation data, noting that headline and core inflation figures came in as expected but remain concerning for everyday consumers. She emphasized that while the Federal Reserve committee is unlikely to take immediate action at the next meeting, the persistent rise in living costs—especially energy prices—continues to outpace wage growth, creating financial strain for many households. She speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Oil prices rise after Trump says U.S. will attack Iran 'very hard'
top_signals.summary: Oil prices rose after the U.S. launched military strikes against Iran, raising concerns that renewed clashes may threaten shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: The quantum clock is ticking: it's Bitcoin's problem, not Ethereum's
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Mastercard prepares for a future where AI agents make payments
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
